intro: The plant × Gonostapelia maculosoides is a hybrid succulent species, belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the 'Queen of the Night' due to its unique nocturnal blooming habit. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The bioactive constituents of × Gonostapelia maculosoides include iridoid glycosides, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, × Gonostapelia maculosoides is often prepared as a dried powder or infused in water to make a medicinal tea, which is believed to promote healing and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
